props.caption: GPT-5.6 shipped through a government-requested preview gate; a voluntary framework is starting to look like the release path

items.note: Two crude tankers were struck and one seafarer killed as US Central Command completed another wave of strikes and escorted traffic kept moving; Washington dropped its 20% transit-fee idea for Gulf investment deals.
Shipping stayed restricted after Ukrainian strikes on Russian vessels and Moscow moved to reroute grain exports, while parliament accepted the prime minister’s resignation and the government fell.
The UN rights chief warned of catastrophe under near-siege conditions, and the EU banned Sudanese gold to choke the war economy as the city braced for the fate that befell El Fasher.
The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak widened as the WHO reported it had received only about 40% of the money it needs and a trial of an experimental antiviral began.
Typhoon Bavi drove more than 260,000 people from their homes across the northeast, the strongest storm to strike the mainland this year.

items.what: Projectiles struck two crude tankers near Hormuz, killing one seafarer, as US strikes continued and escorted traffic kept moving; the president dropped his 20% transit-fee idea for Gulf investment deals, and no fee instrument was ever posted.

OpenAI shipped GPT-5.6 through a preview the US government requested before broad release, tracing to a June White House framework that is voluntary yet increasingly reads as the path top models take to market.

Spain’s Lamine Yamal called football a bridge and Argentina’s war veterans urged fans not to refight the Malvinas, making restraint the loudest political act hours before France meet Spain and a day before England meet Argentina.

An online story that Senator Lindsey Graham was assassinated spread through partisan and foreign accounts before Republican allies and the president dismantled it; his office reported a sudden illness and a preliminary finding of aortic dissection.
The EU banned the purchase and transfer of Sudanese gold and the chemicals used to extract it, aiming at the revenue that funds the war as the UN warned El Obeid could follow El Fasher.
The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the DR Congo widened while the WHO said it had received about 40% of the response funding it needs, and a trial of Gilead’s experimental antiviral began.
